gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Bind two hypervectors (associate)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " For MAP-I/MAP-B/BSC/HRR/FHRR this is an algebraic `Exact` op; for SBC it is `Proven`"] # [doc = " (Bloom analysis тАФ `matrix.rs`).  Both operands must have the same length as the model's"] # [doc = " `dim`; a mismatch is `Err(DimMismatch)`, never a silent coercion (C1 / G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::DimMismatch`] тАФ the operands' lengths disagree with each other or the model."] # [inline] pub fn bind < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , a : & [f64] , b : & [f64]) -> Result < Vec < f64 > , VsaError > { model . bind (a , b) }
gaps.reason: type parameter `M` carries a bound тАФ type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Unbind (recover a factor from a bind product)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Exact for self-inverse models (MAP-I/MAP-B/BSC) and SBC (`Proven`); `Empirical` for"] # [doc = " HRR/FHRR (the approximate inverse is lossy тАФ the residual weak link; RFC-0003 ┬з4.1).  For"] # [doc = " HRR/FHRR the result should be cleaned up against a codebook ([`cleanup`]) to obtain a"] # [doc = " usable item тАФ do not treat a raw unbind result as `Exact`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::DimMismatch`] тАФ the operands' lengths disagree with each other or the model."] # [inline] pub fn unbind < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , c : & [f64] , b : & [f64]) -> Result < Vec < f64 > , VsaError > { model . unbind (c , b) }
gaps.reason: type parameter `M` carries a bound тАФ type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Bundle (superpose) a non-empty set of hypervectors."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The guarantee tag depends on the model (read from `intrinsic_guarantee(Bundle)`):"] # [doc = " `Proven` for MAP-I/MAP-B/BSC/SBC; `Empirical` for HRR/FHRR.  A bundle over zero items is"] # [doc = " `Err(EmptyBundle)`, never a fabricated zero vector (C1 / G2).  MAP-B nested-bundle inputs"] # [doc = " yield `Err(NestedBundleUnsupported)` тАФ reliability decays `1/2 + 1/2^r` with nesting depth"] # [doc = " `r`, so nesting beyond depth 1 is refused explicitly rather than silently degrading accuracy"] # [doc = " (RR-13)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " For a Value-level `Proven` bundle with a checked `CapacityBound` certificate, use the"] # [doc = " Value-level adapters in `mycelium-vsa` directly (e.g. `MapI::bundle_values_certified`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::EmptyBundle`] тАФ zero items supplied."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::NestedBundleUnsupported`] тАФ MAP-B input is itself a MAP-B bundle (RR-13)."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::DimMismatch`] тАФ items have differing lengths."] # [inline] pub fn bundle < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , items : & [& [f64]]) -> Result < Vec < f64 > , VsaError > { model . bundle (items) }
gaps.reason: type parameter `M` carries a bound тАФ type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Permute (cyclically shift) a hypervector by `shift` positions."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Exact` for **all** models (┬з4.1 erratum тАФ a fixed coordinate bijection; RFC-0003 ┬з4.1)."] # [doc = " The inverse is [`unpermute`] with the same `shift`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::DimMismatch`] тАФ operand length disagrees with the model's `dim`."] # [inline] pub fn permute < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , a : & [f64] , shift : i64) -> Result < Vec < f64 > , VsaError > { model . permute (a , shift) }
gaps.reason: type parameter `M` carries a bound тАФ type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The inverse of [`permute`] by the same `shift` тАФ exactly undoes the cyclic rotation."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Exact` for all models (a permutation's inverse is always exact; ┬з4.1 erratum)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::DimMismatch`] тАФ operand length disagrees with the model's `dim`."] # [inline] pub fn unpermute < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , a : & [f64] , shift : i64) -> Result < Vec < f64 > , VsaError > { model . unpermute (a , shift) }
gaps.reason: type parameter `M` carries a bound тАФ type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Cosine similarity of two hypervectors in `[-1, 1]`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Deterministic (a fixed score); `Exact` тАФ the primitive *score* is exact even when it is used"] # [doc = " as a *decision* input (cleanup, resonator per-slot argmax) where the *decision* is"] # [doc = " bounded-lossy.  See FLAG Q2 in `vsa.md ┬з7` for the open question about a"] # [doc = " crosstalk-context bound when used decisionally."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns `0.0` if either operand has zero norm (the model's documented convention)."] # [inline] pub fn similarity < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , a : & [f64] , b : & [f64]) -> f64 { model . similarity (a , b) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " RoleтАУfiller binding: `bind(role, filler)`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A thin alias for [`bind`] that names the operand roles explicitly.  Same guarantee as"] # [doc = " `bind` for the model (see [`bind`] for the guarantee-tag breakdown)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " See [`bind`]."] # [inline] pub fn bind_role < M : VsaModel > (model : & M , role : & [f64] , filler : & [f64] ,) -> Result < Vec < f64 > , VsaError > { bind (model , role , filler) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Cleanup: nearest-atom indexed retrieval against an item memory."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Empirical` (bounded-lossy item memory, Clarkson Thm 16; M-132).  Returns"] # [doc = " `Match { label, index, confidence, margin }` so the caller can inspect and threshold the"] # [doc = " result тАФ never a silent low-confidence guess (C1/C3 / G2 / FR-C2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Explicit refusals (C1 / G2):"] # [doc = " - `Err(BelowCleanupThreshold)` when `confidence < min_confidence` (the caller-supplied floor)."] # [doc = " - `Err(BelowCleanupThreshold)` when `margin < min_margin` (near-tied slots). See the FLAG in"] # [doc = "   the body: `mycelium-vsa::VsaError` has **no** distinct `Ambiguous` variant, so the"] # [doc = "   margin-shortfall case is surfaced through `BelowCleanupThreshold` (with `confidence` carrying"] # [doc = "   the margin and `threshold` carrying `min_margin`) тАФ never a silent guess. A dedicated variant"] # [doc = "   is a kernel change (FLAG)."] # [doc = " - `Err(EmptyCodebook)` when the memory is empty or the query has the wrong length."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Setting `min_confidence = 0.0` and `min_margin = 0.0` disables the threshold gates,"] # [doc = " returning the raw nearest-neighbour hit (the confidence/margin are still reported in"] # [doc = " `Match` for the caller to inspect)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::EmptyCodebook`] тАФ the memory is empty or query length disagrees with `dim`."] # [doc = " - [`VsaError::BelowCleanupThreshold`] тАФ `confidence < min_confidence`, **or** the"] # [doc = "   margin-shortfall case `margin < min_margin` (see the body FLAG: `VsaError` has no"] # [doc = "   `Ambiguous` variant, so both refusals share this variant)."] pub fn cleanup < M : VsaModel > (codebook : & CleanupMemory , query : & [f64] , model : & M , min_confidence : f64 , min_margin : f64 ,) -> Result < Match , VsaError > { let hit = codebook . cleanup (query , model) . ok_or (VsaError :: EmptyCodebook) ? ; if hit . confidence < min_confidence { return Err (VsaError :: BelowCleanupThreshold { confidence : hit . confidence , threshold : min_confidence , }) ; } if hit . margin < min_margin { return Err (VsaError :: BelowCleanupThreshold { confidence : hit . margin , threshold : min_margin , }) ; } Ok (hit) }
gaps.reason: type parameter `M` carries a bound тАФ type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment
